architecture.summary: Codebook features are produced by finetuning pretrained neural networks with vector quantization bottlenecks inserted at each layer (after attention heads or MLP blocks). During forward pass, activation vectors are compared to learned codebook vectors via cosine similarity, and replaced with the sum of the k most similar codes, creating a sparse discrete bottleneck. The discrete codes are learned end-to-end via straight-through estimators during finetuning, combined with the original task loss (cross-entropy for language modeling) and a reconstruction loss. The sparse, discrete codes enable both unsupervised discovery of interpretable features (by examining tokens where codes activate) and causal intervention (by substituting one code for another to steer behavior).

biblio.title: Codebook Features: Sparse and Discrete Interpretability for Neural Networks

claims.statement: Neural networks can operate under an extreme sparse, discrete bottleneck with only modest degradation in performance
On TokFSM finite state machine dataset, attention-only codebook model achieves comparable language modeling loss and next token accuracy to original model
On TokFSM, codebook models identify state codes with average precision of 97.1% at classifying FSM states, compared to best neuron precision of 70.5%

Codebook transformer learns to detect and assign distinct codes to states of a finite state machine, overcoming the superposition problem, with causal evidence: changing state codes in MLP layers shifts the next token distribution toward the target state
On WikiText-103, 24-layer 410M parameter codebook language model with attention codebooks achieves loss and accuracy close to finetuned baseline

Codebook models are better classifiers of simple textual features than individual neurons: codes achieve over 30% higher average precision on regex-based feature classification than best-neuron baselines

Activating topic codes in TinyStories model causes generated text to mention target topics far more frequently: topic code steering achieves 65-100% mention rate across different topics (fire, dragon, slide, flower, baby, princess), compared to baseline mention rates of 0-42.5%

Codebook features identify diverse, disentangled concepts in language models spanning orthography, syntax, word types, events, and topics, enabling intuitive control of model behavior via code activation
